_meta.title: Inventory Method Selection Decision Tree
_meta.description: AI-consumable decision tree for selecting Method A (perpetual) or Method B (periodic) inventory tracking, and inventory valuation method, for Czech accounting entities.
notes: Method B is legally permitted for all entity sizes under ČÚS 015; size guidance below reflects professional best practice only.
FIFO and weighted-average are permitted; LIFO is explicitly prohibited.
Inventory write-downs (OP k zásobám) are never tax-deductible under Act 593/1992 § 27.
Advisor verification required before AI engine relies on this tree for client recommendations.

method_selector.question: Which inventory recording method does the entity use?

options.label: Method A — Průběžné sledování (Perpetual)
conditions: Entity has warehouse management system (WMS) or ERP
Entity is střední or velká účetní jednotka
Entity needs real-time stock balances for management reporting
Entity has multiple warehouses or complex inventory flows

options.label: Method B — Periodické sledování (Periodic)
conditions: Entity is mikro or malá účetní jednotka
Entity has simple inventory operations without WMS
Entity conducts comprehensive year-end physical counts
Entity does not require mid-year balance sheet accuracy on inventory

valuation_method_selector.question: Which unit-cost flow method does the entity apply to inventory consumption?

options.label: FIFO — First-In, First-Out
conditions: Tracking individual cost layers is practical
options.note: Oldest cost layers consumed first. Closing stock = most recent costs.

options.label: Vážený průměr průběžný (Continuous weighted average)
conditions: WMS or ERP supports real-time average recalculation after each receipt
options.note: Average cost recalculated after every receipt event.

options.label: Vážený průměr periodický (Periodic weighted average)
conditions: Entity recalculates average monthly or quarterly
options.note: Average cost computed at end of each period (typically monthly).

options.label: LIFO — Last-In, First-Out
options.note: EXPLICITLY PROHIBITED under Czech accounting law. ČÚS 015 body 5.3 / Decree 500/2002 § 50.

own_production_valuation.question: What cost method is used for valuing own-production inventory (WIP, semi-finished, finished goods)?

options.label: Full absorption cost method (úplné vlastní náklady výroby)
includes: Fixed manufacturing overhead (factory depreciation, facility costs)
excludes: Interest — excluded unless explicit entity-policy opt-in per Decree 500/2002 § 47
options.note: Fixed overhead allocation must use normal capacity base, not actual (idle capacity = period cost).

inventory_impairment.question: When must an inventory write-down (opravná položka) be recognized?
inventory_impairment.legal_basis: Act 593/1992 § 27 — no zákonná opravná položka for zásoby
inventory_impairment.dppo_treatment: Add back to tax base in DPPO return (non-deductible expense via 559)

scenarios.scenario: Natural wastage within entity-defined norm (přirozené úbytky do normy)
scenarios.legal_basis: Act 586/1992 § 24 odst. 1 ZDP (generally deductible as spotřeba — business consumption within documented norms; NOT § 24 odst. 2 písm. l))
scenarios.correction: K2C.3 2026-05-24: within-norm wastage = spotřeba under § 24 odst. 1, not škoda under odst. 2 písm. l)

scenarios.scenario: Shortage exceeding natural wastage norm, cause unknown
Theft confirmed by police report — neznámý pachatel (unknown perpetrator)
scenarios.legal_basis: Act 586/1992 § 24 odst. 2 písm. l) — ztráty způsobené neznámým pachatelem dle potvrzení policie
scenarios.note: Requires police confirmation that perpetrator is unknown. Known-perpetrator theft does NOT qualify — perpetrator is liable for damages.

scenarios.legal_basis: Act 586/1992 § 25 odst. 1 písm. n) — perpetrator should pay damages; not deductible as škoda
scenarios.correction: K2C.3 2026-05-24: known-perpetrator theft does not qualify under § 24 odst. 2 písm. l)

scenarios.scenario: Force majeure (živelná pohroma — fire, flood, etc.)
scenarios.legal_basis: Act 586/1992 § 24 odst. 2 písm. l) — škoda způsobená živelnou pohromou

scenarios.deductible: Deductible up to amount of compensation claim recognized
scenarios.legal_basis: Act 586/1992 § 24 odst. 2 písm. l) (škoda with compensation arrangement)

import_goods_dph.question: How is import DPH treated for goods from third countries?

scenarios.note: Both clo and import DPH flow into pořizovací cena

customs_duty.note: No customs duty for EU goods

import_dph.note: Samovyměření — simultaneous 343 MD and D; net zero for full deduction plátce
